##section:executive_summary
An excavation at the Mock Evaluation site (Project ID: mock_evaluation) yielded a stratified sequence of five contexts, ranging from a modern topsoil horizon to a Late Pleistocene head deposit. The principal objectives were to expose the stratified sequence, record the deposit’s relationships, and recover archaeological remains that would illuminate the site’s historic use. The excavation employed a systematic, grid‑based approach, with a focus on the preservation of the ditch [102] and its fill assemblages. Key findings include a Roman‑period fill [104] containing greyware and animal bone, a secondary fill [103] rich in charcoal, and a modern topsoil [101] that produced post‑medieval pottery and glass. Environmental sampling was conducted on the primary and secondary fills, and a photographic record was maintained to document the stratigraphic narrative. The report presents a phased description of the contexts, a comprehensive finds summary, and a discussion of the site’s significance in its regional context.
